_comment: Phase DX.3b — Per-axis variant seeding registry. The Harness variant picker uses these to seed N parallel LLM calls with distinct design directions rather than just a temperature spread. Each axis has a baseInstruction (system/user prompt addendum applied to every variant in the batch) and an ordered list of perVariantHints. The coordinator appends one hint per variant in index order, so with count=3 on axis=accent-hue the first variant gets hints[0], second hints[1], etc. `surprise-me` has no hints — it falls back to pure temperature-spread behaviour. Labels drive the menu UI. Adding a new axis is a content PR — no rebuild.

axes.label: Surprise me
axes.description: No explicit hint. The coordinator spreads temperature across variants so they differ organically.

axes.label: Accent hue
axes.description: Each variant commits to a different dominant accent colour — cool vs warm vs neutral — while keeping layout + density constant.
axes.baseInstruction: Vary ONLY the accent-colour direction between variants. Everything else (typography scale, spacing, layout) stays close to the original.
perVariantHints: Lean cool-accent: primary accent in the 210–240 hue range (blue / indigo). Neutral surfaces, restrained saturation.
Lean warm-accent: primary accent in the 10–35 hue range (orange / amber / terracotta). Warm undertones in neutrals.
Lean emerald-accent: primary accent in the 140–170 hue range (emerald / teal). Slightly cool neutrals.
Lean violet-accent: primary accent in the 270–295 hue range (violet / magenta). Moody dark surfaces, high accent saturation.

axes.label: Density
axes.description: Vary how tightly information is packed. One compact, one comfortable, one spacious.
axes.baseInstruction: Vary ONLY the information density between variants: padding, line-height, type scale, and gutter sizes. Colour palette and visual style stay consistent.
perVariantHints: Lean compact: tight padding, smaller type, denser tables/lists, minimal whitespace. Information-dense.
Lean comfortable: balanced type scale + padding. Default breathing room. Neither dense nor airy.
Lean spacious: generous whitespace, larger type, ample padding, airy layout. Editorial feel.
Lean editorial: oversized type for hero content, long-form spacing, wide margins. Magazine-style.

axes.label: Motion intensity
axes.description: Vary how lively the UI feels — still / subtle / lively. Never affects gameplay timing.
axes.baseInstruction: Vary ONLY the motion + animation intensity between variants. Never change gameplay timers, physics, or event handlers — this is purely presentational motion.
perVariantHints: Lean near-still: prefer reduced-motion. Fades under 120ms. No scroll animations. Minimal hover effects.
Lean subtle: gentle transitions (150–250ms). Quiet micro-interactions on hover + focus. Small entrance fades.
Lean lively: entrance animations on load, spring easing, visible hover reveals, animated state changes. Still stays under 400ms per transition.
Lean playful: elastic + bounce easing, decorative motion (shimmer, parallax), animated illustrations. Clearly decorative but never distracting from primary task.
